hotspots.name: Sahel
hotspots.description: Region of instability, military coups, and Islamist insurgency. Russian influence growing.

history.precedentDescription: 4 military coups in 3 years (Mali 2020/2021, Burkina Faso 2022, Niger 2023)

hotspots.whyItMatters: Russian influence expanding in former French sphere; jihadist groups gaining territory; migration pressure on Europe

hotspots.name: Port-au-Prince
hotspots.description: Gang violence, government collapse, international security mission.

history.precedentDescription: Repeated state collapse (2004 Aristide, 2021 Moïse assassination, 2024 gang takeover)

hotspots.whyItMatters: Humanitarian catastrophe at US doorstep; migration surge potential; test of African-led peacekeeping

hotspots.name: Horn of Africa
hotspots.description: Resurgent piracy, Al-Shabaab activity, Ethiopia-Somaliland port dispute.

history.precedentDescription: Ethiopia-Eritrea war, Tigray war, Somali civil war, Sudan coups, piracy waves

hotspots.whyItMatters: Bab el-Mandeb chokepoint security; 12% of global trade at risk; Red Sea shipping rerouting

hotspots.name: Pakistan–Afghanistan Border
hotspots.description: Ongoing conflict along the Pak–Afghan border. Pakistan military operations against TTP; cross-border strikes and border closures. Tensions with Afghan Taliban over border security.

history.precedentDescription: Recurring border crises, TTP resurgence post-2021, militant sanctuaries in Afghanistan

hotspots.whyItMatters: Nuclear-armed state at contested border; regional stability; displacement and humanitarian impact

hotspots.name: DC
hotspots.description: US government and military headquarters. Intelligence community center.

hotspots.name: Silicon Valley
hotspots.description: Global tech center. AI development hub. Major economic indicator.

hotspots.name: Wall Street
hotspots.description: Global financial center. Market movements. Fed policy.

hotspots.name: Houston
hotspots.description: Energy sector HQ. NASA mission control. Space industry.

hotspots.name: Moscow
hotspots.description: Russian Federation command center. Military operations hub.
hotspots.whyItMatters: Nuclear power at war; energy leverage over Europe; global order revisionism

hotspots.name: Beijing
hotspots.description: Chinese Communist Party headquarters. PLA command center.

history.precedentDescription: Tiananmen 1989, Hong Kong crackdown 2020, COVID lockdowns 2022

hotspots.whyItMatters: Largest economy by PPP; primary US strategic competitor; Taiwan contingency risk

hotspots.name: Kyiv
hotspots.description: Active conflict zone. NATO support operations.

history.cyclicalRisk: Spring/summer offensive season, Winter energy attacks

hotspots.whyItMatters: Largest European war since WWII; NATO Article 5 test; global food/energy security

hotspots.name: Taipei
hotspots.description: Taiwan Strait tensions. Semiconductor supply chain.

history.cyclicalRisk: US-Taiwan political visits, PRC National Day (Oct 1)

hotspots.whyItMatters: TSMC produces 90% of advanced chips; conflict would devastate global tech supply chains

hotspots.name: Tehran
hotspots.description: Iranian nuclear program. Regional proxy operations.

history.precedentDescription: Revolution 1979, Iran-Iraq War, JCPOA negotiations, Soleimani assassination

hotspots.whyItMatters: Near-nuclear threshold state; controls Strait of Hormuz; Axis of Resistance coordinator

hotspots.name: Tel Aviv
hotspots.description: Military operations. Regional security. Intelligence activities.

history.precedentDescription: 1948 War, 1967 Six-Day War, 1973 Yom Kippur, Lebanon 2006, Gaza wars
history.cyclicalRisk: Jewish holidays, Ramadan tensions, election cycles

hotspots.whyItMatters: Regional escalation risk to multi-front war; US treaty ally; Iran confrontation flashpoint

hotspots.name: Pyongyang
hotspots.description: Nuclear weapons program. Missile testing. Cyber operations.

history.precedentDescription: Korean War 1950-53, nuclear tests 2006-2017, Trump summits, missile tests ongoing

hotspots.whyItMatters: Nuclear-armed hermit state; ICBM can reach US mainland; cyber threat actor; Russia military supplier

hotspots.name: London
hotspots.description: UK intelligence headquarters. Five Eyes member.

hotspots.name: Brussels
hotspots.description: NATO alliance headquarters. European Union center.

hotspots.name: Caracas
hotspots.description: Political crisis. Economic sanctions. Regional instability.

hotspots.name: Mexico City
hotspots.description: Cartel warfare, fentanyl trafficking, military deployments, state fragility in multiple regions.

history.precedentDescription: Decades of cartel wars (Sinaloa vs CJNG, Zetas era, Calderón drug war 2006, El Chapo captures)
history.cyclicalRisk: Election cycles trigger violence; cartel leadership changes spark turf wars

hotspots.whyItMatters: Largest US land border; fentanyl crisis killing 100k+ Americans/year; regional destabilization; migration driver

hotspots.name: Nuuk
hotspots.description: Arctic strategic territory. US military presence, sovereignty questions.

hotspots.name: Riyadh
hotspots.description: Saudi Arabia power center. OPEC+ decisions. Regional influence.

hotspots.name: Cairo
hotspots.description: Egyptian command. Gaza border control. Suez Canal security.

hotspots.name: Baghdad
hotspots.description: Iraqi government. Iran-backed militias. US military presence.

hotspots.name: Damascus
hotspots.description: Syrian civil war aftermath. Multiple foreign interventions.

hotspots.name: Doha
hotspots.description: Qatar diplomatic hub. US CENTCOM base. Al Jazeera HQ.

hotspots.name: Ankara
hotspots.description: NATO member. Kurdish conflict. Syria/Libya operations.

hotspots.name: Beirut
hotspots.description: Lebanon crisis. Hezbollah stronghold. Israel border tensions.

hotspots.name: Sana'a
hotspots.description: Yemen conflict. Houthi Red Sea attacks. Shipping disruption.

history.precedentDescription: Civil war since 2014, Saudi intervention 2015, US strikes 2024

hotspots.whyItMatters: Disrupting 12% of global trade via Suez; insurance costs spiking; Iran proxy demonstration

hotspots.name: Abu Dhabi
hotspots.description: UAE strategic hub. Regional military operations.

conflicts.name: Iran War Theater
conflicts.description: Joint US-Israeli military operation (US: Operation Epic Fury / Israel: Operation Roaring Lion). 1000+ targets struck including military, nuclear, and leadership sites. Supreme Leader Khamenei killed in Tehran strikes. Iran retaliating with missiles and drones across the region.
keyDevelopments: Iranian missile/drone retaliation on Gulf states

conflicts.name: Strait of Hormuz Crisis
conflicts.location: Strait of Hormuz & Persian Gulf Approaches
conflicts.description: Iran attempting to close Strait of Hormuz. IRGC naval/air operations active. Three tankers damaged. US sank 9 Iranian warships. GPS spoofing/jamming reported. 20-30% of global oil/gas transits through this chokepoint. Brent crude spiking to $80+.

conflicts.name: Ukraine War
conflicts.description: Full-scale Russian invasion of Ukraine. Active frontlines in Donetsk, Luhansk, Zaporizhzhia, and Kherson oblasts. Heavy artillery, drone warfare, and trench combat.

conflicts.name: Gaza Conflict
conflicts.description: Israeli military operations in Gaza following October 7 attacks. Ground invasion, aerial bombardment. Humanitarian crisis. Regional escalation with Hezbollah.

conflicts.name: Israel-Lebanon Border
conflicts.description: Cross-border artillery and rocket fire. Targeted assassinations. High risk of full-scale escalation.

conflicts.name: Red Sea Crisis
conflicts.location: Red Sea & Gulf of Aden, Yemen
conflicts.description: Houthi maritime campaign against commercial shipping. US/UK airstrikes on Houthi targets. Ongoing blockade attempts.

conflicts.name: Sudan Civil War
conflicts.description: Power struggle between SAF and RSF paramilitary has engulfed the entire country. RSF controls most of Darfur and Khartoum; SAF holds Port Sudan and eastern regions. World's largest displacement crisis. Famine conditions in multiple states.

conflicts.name: Myanmar Civil War
conflicts.description: Civil war following 2021 military coup. Resistance forces and ethnic armed organizations have captured significant territory. Junta losing control of border regions. Multiple coordinated offensives ongoing.

conflicts.name: Korean Demilitarized Zone
conflicts.description: 250km-long, 4km-wide buffer zone along the Military Demarcation Line established by the 1953 Korean Armistice Agreement. Despite its name, one of the most heavily militarized borders in the world.

conflicts.name: Pakistan–Afghanistan Border Conflict
conflicts.location: Pakistan–Afghanistan border (KPK, Balochistan, Federally Administered Tribal Areas)
conflicts.description: Escalating tensions along the Pakistan–Afghanistan border. Pakistan has conducted cross-border strikes targeting TTP sanctuaries in Afghan territory, prompting border closures and diplomatic friction with the Taliban government. Long-running dispute over militant safe havens and border security.
